solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def isPowerOfFour(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n & 0x55555555) != 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses bitwise operations to check if `n` is a power of four. First, it checks if `n` is a power of two using `(n & (n - 1)) == 0`. Then, it ensures that `n` is a power of four by checking if only one bit is set and it is in an odd position using `(n & 0x55555555) != 0`. This approach avoids loops or recursion and is very efficient.
